summary: Frontend testing of manage-roles page after claimed fix. CRITICAL BUG STILL EXISTS: Page gets stuck in infinite loading state when navigating from Administration tab. The fix (adding fallback timer and adjusted loading conditions at lines 108-122, 330-338) was NOT effective. However, the permission hierarchy logic (full/sub-permissions) works correctly when page is accessed via direct URL navigation.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Loading state bug NOT FIXED. Page shows 'جاري التحميل...' indefinitely when navigating via in-app navigation (Administration -> Roles Management). API calls complete successfully (GET /roles and GET /permissions/available return 200), but component remains in loading state. Works only via direct URL or page reload.
data-testid='add-role-btn' attribute not being rendered in HTML output. TouchableOpacity with data-testid doesn't translate to HTML attribute in React Native Web.

integration_issues.issue: Navigation from Administration tab to Roles Management causes infinite loading. Hypothesis: The component re-mounts and the useEffect dependencies (authLoading, user, fetchData) don't trigger data fetch correctly during SPA navigation.

action_items: CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: The loading state issue persists. Debug WHY setLoading(false) in fetchData() finally block doesn't update the UI when navigating via expo-router
Add console.log before setLoading(false) to verify it's being called
Consider removing the loading condition check entirely and showing content immediately while data loads
Alternative: Use a ref to track if component is mounted before setting state
Check if expo-router navigation causes component unmount/remount issues
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Permission hierarchy logic WORKS: togglePermission() correctly handles full permission selection (adds all sub-permissions) and sub-permission deselection (removes full permission)
✓ UI shows 'مفعّل تلقائياً' (auto-enabled) and lock icon for locked sub-permissions when full permission is selected
✗ Loading state bug NOT FIXED despite code changes at lines 108-122, 330-338
✗ Fallback timer (2 seconds) doesn't resolve the issue - page stays loading even after 8+ seconds
✗ The condition `loading && roles.length === 0` should be false after API returns data, but it's not updating

success_rate.frontend: 40% - Loading bug blocks in-app navigation. Permission logic works via direct URL access.

context_for_next_testing_agent: CRITICAL UNRESOLVED BUG: manage-roles.tsx infinite loading persists. Tested scenarios: 1) In-app navigation (FAIL - infinite loading), 2) Direct URL after login (PASS - works correctly), 3) Permission hierarchy (PASS - full permission selects sub-permissions, locked sub-permissions show correctly). The API calls succeed (visible in console logs) but React state doesn't update. Suspect expo-router navigation causing component lifecycle issues.

roles_page_via_in_app_navigation.details: Page stuck in loading state after navigating Administration -> Roles Management

roles_page_via_direct_url.details: Page loads correctly when navigating directly to /manage-roles after login

add_role_modal.details: Modal opens correctly when clicking blue + button. Shows role name, description, and permissions sections

full_permission_auto_selects_sub_permiss.details: Clicking 'إدارة كاملة للأقسام' automatically selects and locks sub-permissions (عرض الأقسام, etc.)

locked_sub_permissions_display.details: Sub-permissions show lock icon and 'مفعّل تلقائياً' text when full permission is selected

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The loading state issue appears to be related to React Native Web/Expo Router navigation lifecycle. When navigating via Link or router.push(), the component re-mounts but the state updates from fetchData() don't trigger a re-render. The API calls complete (confirmed via console logs) but setLoading(false) and setRoles() don't update the UI.
reproduction_steps: 3. Click on 'الإدارة' (Administration) tab in bottom navigation
5. Page shows 'جاري التحميل...' and never loads content
6. Alternative: Navigate directly to /manage-roles - works correctly
potential_fixes: 1. Use useFocusEffect from @react-navigation/native to fetch data when screen is focused
2. Add isMounted ref check before setState calls
3. Use zustand or context for global state instead of local useState
4. Debug with React DevTools to see if component is actually re-rendering
